diagrams.title: Odylith Product Governance Loop
diagrams.summary: Odylith governs its own product backlog, plans, bugs, diagrams, and generated surfaces inside the public repo while consumers keep their own repo truth locally.

components.name: odylith
components.description: Top-level product contract that owns the installable CLI, governance loop, and self-governing repo boundary.

components.name: dashboard
components.description: Shell surface that links the Odylith governance views into one operator-facing product entrypoint.

components.name: radar
components.description: Backlog/workstream surface that tracks active product slices and ranking decisions for Odylith itself.

components.name: atlas
components.description: Diagram surface that stores and renders the architecture maps governing the Odylith product.

components.name: compass
components.description: Execution-audit surface that records and renders Odylith timeline evidence for its own repo.

components.name: registry
components.description: Component evidence surface that ties Odylith product components to specs, workstreams, and conversation traces.

components.name: casebook
components.description: Bug and learning surface that captures Odylith product incidents and corrective follow-through.

diagrams.title: Odylith Context And Agent Execution Stack
diagrams.summary: The context engine, memory layer, and agent-routing/orchestration stack collaborate to ground tasks before leaf execution or escalation.

components.name: odylith-context-engine
components.description: Grounding engine that builds deterministic packets from repo truth before implementation and governance actions.

components.name: subagent-router
components.description: Bounded leaf-routing layer that decides whether one grounded subtask should stay local or delegate.

components.name: subagent-orchestrator
components.description: Prompt-level orchestration layer that decomposes grounded work into safe serial or parallel leaves.

diagrams.title: Odylith Diagnosis And Remediation Loop
diagrams.summary: Tribunal diagnoses grounded dossiers and Remediator turns approved outcomes into bounded correction packets with explicit validation and rollback posture.

components.name: tribunal
components.description: Diagnosis engine that weighs grounded evidence and produces ranked explanations, risks, and next checks.

components.name: remediator
components.description: Correction-packet compiler that turns approved diagnoses into bounded execution, validation, and rollback instructions.

diagrams.title: Odylith Delivery Governance Topology
diagrams.summary: Topology view of the Odylith observer, Tribunal reasoning engine, and Remediator packet flow showing case ranking, approval and clearance state, runtime v4 artifacts, and native-evidence non-Odylith surfaces including Casebook for bug history.

components.name: Odylith
components.description: Observer daemon and control-plane surface that owns the full operator queue, approvals, recommendations, and verification.

components.name: Registry
components.description: Component intelligence surface that stays native to component evidence, spec traceability, and forensic timeline.

components.name: Casebook
components.description: Bug knowledge surface that stays native to bug history, root cause, solution, verification, and prevention evidence.

components.name: Compass
components.description: Timeline evidence source that stays native to execution chronology, current workstreams, and risk framing.

components.name: Radar
components.description: Workstream requirement and success-metric surface that stays native to backlog evidence and traceability.

components.name: Atlas
components.description: Architecture surface that stays native to topology, freshness, and linked engineering artifacts.

components.name: Dashboard
components.description: Shared tooling shell that owns the top-3 Operator Inbox drawer and deep-link coherence across governance surfaces.

diagrams.title: Odylith Delivery Intelligence Closed Loop
diagrams.summary: Sequence view of the Observe -> Correlate -> Triage -> Tribunal -> Packetize -> Await Approval -> Apply or Delegate -> Await Clearance loop after the B-062 redesign.

components.name: Odylith
components.description: Continuous observer loop that owns delta detection, correlation, posture persistence, queue refresh, and surface refresh.

components.name: Registry
components.description: Supplies component scope and proof routing context for queue items that land on live components.

components.name: Compass
components.description: Receives verification evidence and contributes timeline proof for queue items.

components.name: Dashboard
components.description: Hosts the tabbed operator surfaces plus the top-ranked shell inbox refreshed after posture changes.

diagrams.title: Odylith Operator Inbox and Evidence Routing
diagrams.summary: Focused routing diagram showing how Odylith publishes ranked case briefs and approval-gated packet actions to the shell inbox and Odylith, while the other surfaces stay native to timeline, topology, traceability, component evidence, and bug evidence.

components.name: Operator queue
components.description: Ranked interventions with why_now, do_this_now, success_check, and proof_highlights.

components.name: Shell inbox
components.description: Viewport-owned top-3 drawer that surfaces ranked interventions and deep-links into Odylith.

components.name: Native evidence surfaces
components.description: Radar, Registry, Casebook, Atlas, and Compass stay native to their own evidence model and only route operators into Odylith when deeper intervention detail is needed.

diagrams.title: Odylith Tribunal Multi-Actor Reasoning Engine
diagrams.summary: Detailed Tribunal anatomy showing dossier intake, the full actor inventory, adjudication/editor flow, and the guardrails that keep Odylith briefs grounded and non-generic.

components.name: Grounded dossier intake
components.description: Decision scope, named artifacts, timestamps, components, surfaces, approvals, and clearance state are normalized into one grounded Tribunal dossier.

components.name: Independent actor pass
components.description: Observer, ownership, causal, policy, normative, adversary, counterfactual, gap, risk, and prescriber all examine the same dossier before adjudication.

components.name: Adjudication and editorial merge
components.description: The adjudicator chooses the leading and rival explanations, then the editor compresses that into one maintainer-facing decision brief plus systemic context.

components.name: Reasoning guardrails
components.description: Grounding, non-generic output, self-reference downgrade, proof routing, and fingerprint-gated cache reuse constrain the actor system.

diagrams.title: Odylith Tribunal Dossier and Remediation Lifecycle
diagrams.summary: Detailed lifecycle view of dossier assembly, actor adjudication, packet compilation, approval, execution or delegation, and final clearance update.

components.name: Dossier builder
components.description: Delivery intelligence assembles one grounded case dossier with repo objects, checkpoints, approvals, and evidence before the actor pass starts.

components.name: Tribunal adjudication
components.description: Actor memos are merged into one leading explanation, one rival explanation, and an editorial brief rather than leaking raw actor chatter to Odylith.

components.name: Remediator packet engine
components.description: Executable prescriptions become bounded deterministic, AI-engine, hybrid, or manual packets with fingerprints, validation, and rollback semantics.

components.name: Approval and clearance loop
components.description: Operator approval, execution or delegation, stale checks, and clearance updates drive the final posture back into Odylith runtime state.

diagrams.title: Odylith Delivery Intelligence Signal Journal and Observer Pipeline
diagrams.summary: End-to-end view of Odylith signal intake, runtime signal journaling, observer posture build, delivery-intelligence scope synthesis, and the runtime artifacts that feed the maintainer-facing control plane.

components.name: Signal sources
components.description: Repo worktree changes, Compass timeline events, and runtime-local build/CI/deploy/precommit signals all feed Odylith's observer loop.

components.name: Signal Journal
components.description: The runtime-local `events.v1.jsonl` journal records normalized non-Compass signals so the observer loop can correlate them on each refresh.

components.name: Observer and scope synthesis
components.description: The watcher builds the cheap posture shell, then Delivery Intelligence synthesizes the full scope universe and marks live-actionable scopes before Tribunal starts.

components.name: Runtime truth and consumers
components.description: Odylith persists posture, reasoning, delivery, and decision-ledger artifacts that drive the CLI, Odylith UI, Atlas proof routes, and full sync workflows.

diagrams.title: Odylith Tribunal Selection Funnel and Queue Formation
diagrams.summary: Selection-funnel view showing how the full Delivery Intelligence scope universe narrows through live-actionability, Tribunal eligibility, scenario coverage, and priority fill into the bounded maintainer queue.

components.name: Observed scope universe
components.description: Delivery Intelligence synthesizes workstream, component, diagram, surface, and grid scopes before Tribunal begins narrowing them.

components.name: Eligibility gate
components.description: Tribunal only considers live-actionable workstreams/components/diagrams whose primary scenario is not `clear_path`.

components.name: Selection contract
components.description: Tribunal ranks candidates, covers distinct scenario classes first, then fills remaining slots by priority while keeping stable tie-break semantics explicit.

components.name: Queue rationale output
components.description: The bounded queue carries both queue-level funnel stats and per-case inclusion rationale so truncation remains auditable.

diagrams.title: Odylith Tribunal Adjudication, Editorial Merge, and Packet Assembly
diagrams.summary: Detailed view of how one selected Tribunal case moves from grounded dossier to actor memos, adjudication, provider validation, editorial merge, packet compilation, and control-plane feedback through approval and clearance.

components.name: Grounded dossier and actor pass
components.description: Tribunal builds one dossier and runs the full actor lattice over the same evidence before any editorial merge occurs.

components.name: Adjudication and provider validation
components.description: The adjudicator chooses the leading story and bounded provider refinements are accepted only when they validate against named evidence ids.

components.name: Editorial merge
components.description: Tribunal compresses the adjudicated outcome into a short queue headline and lead brief while keeping systemic and rival/risk context as separate outputs.

components.name: Packet assembly and feedback loop
components.description: Remediator compiles the bounded packet and Odylith later folds approval, delegation, apply, and clearance state back into posture.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Router Decision and Escalation Flow
diagrams.summary: Decision flow for bounded subagent routing beneath the prompt-level orchestrator: intake, measured Odylith control-advisory assessment, graph-backed architecture-execution guards, confidence-backed profile selection, native spawn/close payload emission, rescope-vs-escalate outcomes, and family-aware adaptive tuning.

components.name: Route intake and assessment
components.description: The router normalizes bounded prompt metadata, scores ambiguity/risk/scope/validation signals, and assigns task family plus route confidence before delegation.

components.name: Measured control-advisory layer
components.description: The router now reads one bounded Odylith advisory contract compiled from the local evaluation ledger, carrying measured state/confidence plus depth/delegation/parallelism/budget/retrieval/speed posture, and also respects architecture-dossier execution hints when topology grounding is weak or high-risk.

components.name: Profile selection, confidence backstop, and xhigh gate
components.description: Weighted profile scoring chooses among Spark and GPT-5.4 tiers, low-confidence writes can be promoted to stronger GPT-5.4 profiles, grounded high-risk architecture slices can earn the strongest bounded lane, and `xhigh` remains separately gated by critical risk or maximum-accuracy feature conditions.

components.name: Validation, rescope, and adaptive loop
components.description: One spawned subagent is validated explicitly, may escalate once, may instead return control to the main thread for local rescope, and now emits native spawn-agent plus closeout payloads that keep the host thread aligned with the routed contract.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Router Governance and Audit Topology
diagrams.summary: Governance topology for the Subagent Router as the bounded leaf-routing layer beneath the Subagent Orchestrator, including thin skills, route-confidence outputs, native spawn/close payloads, the local evaluation-to-advisory control loop, Compass audit stream, Registry manifest, living spec, runbook, and Atlas coverage.

components.name: Operator and execution surface
components.description: The main agent or maintainer invokes the thin skill and router script, which choose one worker profile for bounded delegation and emit route-confidence plus native host-call payloads.

components.name: Local state and forensic audit
components.description: Local evaluation and tuning state plus optional Compass timeline events preserve explainability, measured control advisories, family-aware adaptation, and feedback without creating tracked runtime truth.

components.name: Registry and Atlas governance
components.description: The component manifest, living spec, runbook, and diagram metadata make the routing policy inspectable across Registry, Atlas, Compass, and the tooling shell as a governed AI execution surface.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Orchestrator Prompt Triage and Routing Flow
diagrams.summary: Prompt-level orchestration flow from an evidence-grounded repo-work prompt across the consumer lane, pinned dogfood, and detached `source-local` maintainer-dev posture through measured Odylith control advisories, architecture-dossier execution hints, local-only gates, orchestration-mode selection, and per-leaf handoff into the bounded Subagent Router, including native spawn and closeout payloads for host execution.

components.name: Evidence grounding and request shaping
components.description: The main thread grounds the evidence cone first, then converts the substantive grounded repo-work prompt into an orchestration request with candidate paths, acceptance criteria, validation commands, and accuracy posture.

components.name: Prompt triage and orchestration mode selection
components.description: The orchestrator evaluates hard local-only gates, measured control advisories, architecture execution hints, and conservative parallel-safety rules to choose `local_only`, `single_leaf`, `serial_batch`, or `parallel_batch`.

components.name: Leaf-routing handoff
components.description: Every delegated leaf is routed through Subagent Router for explicit model plus reasoning selection, while the orchestrator keeps merge ownership and now carries native spawn-agent plus closeout payloads, compact control-advisory posture, and architecture risk/fanout hints in the main-thread execution plan.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Orchestrator Safe Fan-Out and Parallel Safety
diagrams.summary: Parallel-safety decision tree for automatic orchestration, including read-only fan-out caps, disjoint-write checks, coupled-path downgrades, native per-leaf spawn/close payloads, and the local fallback path when any leaf becomes unsafe.

components.name: Safety classification
components.description: The orchestrator classifies grounded prompts into `read_only_safe`, `disjoint_write_safe`, `serial_ordered`, or `local_only` based on path ownership, criticality, and validation clarity.

components.name: Conservative batch shaping
components.description: Read-only work may split into a small parallel batch, disjoint writes may split into a smaller capped batch, and coupled docs/tests/skills/contracts/tooling work is downgraded to serial or local execution.

components.name: Leaf fallback and safety override
components.description: Even after prompt-level decomposition, any leaf-router `main_thread` refusal collapses the whole orchestration plan back to local rescope instead of forcing unsafe fan-out, and accepted leaves still carry native spawn/close payloads for the main thread.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Orchestrator Merge Barrier and Adjudication Ownership
diagrams.summary: Main-thread ownership model for serial and parallel orchestration, covering dependency barriers, cross-leaf merge responsibility, local rescope fallbacks, native closeout payload application, and the rule that architectural adjudication never belongs to a worker leaf.

components.name: Serial dependency ownership
components.description: Ordered leaves expose explicit dependency ids so the main thread controls when the next leaf may start and whether earlier results require local correction first.

components.name: Parallel merge barrier
components.description: Parallel leaves run only on disjoint owned paths, but the main thread still owns merge, final validation, cross-leaf acceptance, and delegated-leaf closeout before any result is treated as landed.

components.name: Rescope and adjudication fallback
components.description: If a leaf route or escalation reveals broader coordination, design conflict, or unclear ownership, control returns to the main thread rather than bouncing the ambiguity between workers.

diagrams.title: Odylith Subagent Orchestrator Governance and Adaptive Learning Topology
diagrams.summary: Governance topology for automatic orchestration across repo policy, thin skills, Registry and Atlas linkage, native host payload contracts, local adaptive learning state, and optional Compass audit so prompt-level delegation remains inspectable and bounded.

components.name: Policy and operator surface
components.description: Root agent policy, agent guidelines, runbooks, and thin skills define when orchestration is automatic, when routing remains leaf-only, and how maintainers should interpret native spawn/close payloads alongside commit-ready vs clean-snapshot proof.

components.name: Local adaptive state and audit
components.description: The orchestrator stores local-only replay-safe tuning by mode and task family, and may append Compass runtime events without turning local adaptation into tracked repo truth.

components.name: Registry and Atlas governance
components.description: The Registry manifest, living specs, runbooks, and diagram catalog make automatic orchestration a first-class governed component linked to the router, Atlas, Registry, and shell evidence surfaces.

diagrams.title: Odylith Installable Product Layered Topology
diagrams.summary: Layered topology for the Odylith product showing tracked repo truth, explicit local memory and retrieval, governance and evaluation engines, governed surfaces, and optional remote augmentation without consumer-specific product branches.

components.name: Tracked product truth and local state
components.description: Keeps repo-owned Radar, Atlas, Casebook, technical plans, contracts, and docs separate from mutable `.odylith/` runtime state.

components.name: Local-first product layers
components.description: Shows the install/CLI, context engine, memory, governance, evaluation, agent-control, and surface-runtime layers that compose the Odylith product.

components.name: Optional remote augmentation
components.description: Makes sparse recall and control attachments explicit as optional edges rather than hidden requirements for local-first operation.

diagrams.title: Odylith Installable Product Bootstrap and Validation Flow
diagrams.summary: Bootstrap and validation flow for Odylith showing consumer-profile resolution, local-first runtime initialization, surface materialization, and the proof gates that keep install and sync honest.

components.name: Bootstrap lane
components.description: Shows the `odylith install` or `odylith sync` path through consumer-profile resolution and runtime bootstrap.

components.name: Governed surface materialization
components.description: Shows where governance, evaluation, and agent-control evidence land in the rendered product surfaces.

components.name: Validation gates
components.description: Makes the product proof boundary explicit so surface coherence, packet contracts, and execution payloads are checked together.

diagrams.title: Odylith Product Runtime Boundary Map
diagrams.summary: Runtime boundary map for Odylith showing how product truth, runtime modules, generated surfaces, `.odylith` state, and the separate runtime/write/validation boundaries fit together.

components.name: Product truth inputs
components.description: Names the product-owned source roots and contracts that drive the runtime instead of flattening them into one generic repo box.

components.name: Runtime module boundary
components.description: Separates context, memory, governance, evaluation, orchestration, and surface modules as the live executable product boundary.

components.name: Generated outputs and local state
components.description: Shows how materialized `odylith/` surfaces and mutable `.odylith/` state differ while still feeding the same governed runtime loop.

diagrams.title: Odylith Self-Host Runtime And Release Gate
diagrams.summary: Self-host posture map showing the installed consumer lane, the Odylith product repo's maintainer mode with pinned dogfood versus detached source-local dev posture, the separate runtime/write/validation boundaries, and the release gate that only accepts pinned dogfood proof.

components.name: Pinned dogfood lane
components.description: Shows the main product repo operating against its tracked pinned runtime rather than implicitly running from a live checkout.

components.name: Detached dev lane
components.description: Makes source-local work an explicit separate override with loud release-ineligible posture instead of a hidden default.

components.name: Release gate and readouts
components.description: Shows the source-only validator and the shell and Compass self-host readouts that expose product-repo drift.

diagrams.title: Odylith Canonical Release Version Session And Publish Lane
diagrams.summary: Canonical maintainer release lane showing preview and sticky session resolution, commit-bound dispatch into the GitHub workflow, signed asset publication, and the post-publish dogfood plus consumer proof sequence.

components.name: Sticky release session
components.description: Shows how one local session version and head SHA are resolved once and then reused across preflight and dispatch.

components.name: Canonical publish lane
components.description: Shows the repo, actor, branch, clean-worktree, and commit-binding guards that keep the maintained release lane authoritative.

components.name: Post-publish proof
components.description: Shows the dogfood activation and consumer rehearsal steps that turn a published version into a credible release candidate.

diagrams.title: Odylith Managed Runtime Release And Install Flow
diagrams.summary: Supported-platform release and install flow showing managed runtime bundle publication, signed verification, and strict interpreter separation between Odylith and the consumer repo toolchain.

components.name: Managed runtime bundles
components.description: Canonical releases ship platform-specific Odylith-managed Python runtimes for macOS and Linux.

components.name: Installer verification
components.description: The hosted installer selects the current supported platform, verifies signed assets, and stages the runtime under .odylith.

components.name: Interpreter boundary
components.description: Odylith commands run inside .odylith while consumer repo commands stay on the consumer toolchain with no shell-level switching.

diagrams.title: Odylith Benchmark Proof And Publication Lane
diagrams.summary: Maps the benchmark corpus, the scoped clean-room snapshot used by the live matched pair, validator-truth restore, conservative publication logic, and the README or release outputs that Odylith uses to prove Codex-on value honestly.

components.name: Benchmark
components.description: Benchmark runner, corpus, conservative publication view, and graph pipeline that turn Odylith benchmark data into release-safe proof.

components.name: Odylith Context Engine
components.description: Grounding and packet runtime whose real task behavior is measured by the benchmark corpus.

components.name: Subagent Orchestrator
components.description: Delegation and execution posture layer whose grounded behavior is a core benchmark subject.
